name: OmopDiagnosticReportObservationView
title: DiagnosticReport → observation (flat)
description: When a DiagnosticReport's LOINC code resolves to an OMOP concept with domain_id = Observation, the report produces one or more observation rows. This applies primarily to clinical document types -- history and physical notes, discharge summaries, evaluation and plan notes, consultation notes -- where the LOINC code represents a document class rather than a quantitative measurement or procedure. Each conclusionCode entry generates a separate row. This is the most frequently hit domain for DiagnosticReport resources in real-world EHR data (clinical notes are far more common than lab panel-level reports).

column.name: person_id
column.description: Resolve Patient/{id} reference to integer person_id. Skip row if unresolvable.

column.name: observation_concept_id
column.description: LOINC code looked up in OMOP vocabulary. Must have domain_id = Observation. Use first LOINC coding found. 0 if concept not found.

column.name: observation_date
column.description: Date component of effectiveDateTime, or effectivePeriod.start. Skip row if absent (ETL-German behavior).

column.name: observation_datetime
column.description: Full timestamp from effectiveDateTime or effectivePeriod.start.

column.name: observation_type_concept_id
column.description: ETL-German maps category via SOURCE_VOCABULARY_ID_DIAGNOSTIC_REPORT_CATEGORY custom concept map. Common defaults: 32817 (EHR) or 32856 (Lab result).

column.name: value_as_string
column.description: ETL-German stores the SNOMED code string here (line 409). Recommended: use conclusionCode display text, or fall back to conclusion free text (truncated to 60 chars).

column.name: value_as_concept_id
column.description: SNOMED conclusion code resolved to OMOP concept. ETL-German currently stores this in observation_source_concept_id instead. Recommended: store the resolved SNOMED concept here as the clinical value of the report.

column.name: provider_id
column.description: First performer reference resolved to provider_id. Also consider resultsInterpreter[0].

column.name: observation_source_value
column.description: Verbatim SNOMED code from conclusionCode. ETL-German stores this (line 405).

column.name: observation_source_concept_id
column.description: SNOMED conclusionCode resolved to OMOP concept ID. ETL-German stores the SNOMED concept here (line 404).

column.name: value_source_value
column.description: Verbatim SNOMED code. ETL-German does not explicitly set this field. Recommended: store the raw conclusionCode string.

where.description: DiagnosticReport.code (LOINC) resolves to OMOP concept with domain_id = Observation
